seoTitle: Pacific Heights vs Pacific Pines — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pacific Heights and Pacific Pines. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pacific Heights vs Pacific Pines: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pacific Heights and Pacific Pines.
heading: Pacific Heights vs Pacific Pines — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pacific Heights, Queensland and Pacific Pines, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pacific Pines has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Pacific Heights (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pacific Heights or Pacific Pines safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pacific Heights has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Pacific Pines scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pacific Heights scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Pacific Pines scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pacific Heights or Pacific Pines?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pacific Pines has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
